channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Georgia native, former President Jimmy Carter's influence stretched into Kern County
item.description: Jimmy Carter's single term as president and his remarkable and lengthy post-presidency left a mark on the nation in ways many may not appreciate today. But some here in Bakersfield most certainly did grasp his impact.
item.dc:creator: Robert Price, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fight over spending stopgap foreshadows funding challenges in new Congress
item.description: The messy government shutdown fight this month foreshadows some of the challenges House Republicans could face next year, as a major test on funding awaits the incoming GOP “trifecta.” Congress narrowly averted a government shutdown last week — but not without a bit of drama.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former President Jimmy Carter dies at 100
item.description: Carter, who served as the 39th president of the United States from 1977 to 1981, died Sunday in Plains, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Kansas once required voters to prove citizenship. That didn't work out so well
item.description: Republicans made claims about illegal voting by noncitizens a centerpiece of their 2024 campaign messaging.
item.dc:creator: John Hanna, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: European officials pitch new idea to shore up defenses with Trump's return
item.description: European officials are struggling to agree on a plan to shore up hundreds of billions of dollars to bolster defenses. Some NATO experts are proposing a global defense bank.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why Trump is targeting Panama, Greenland, Canada
item.description: President-elect Trump has spent parts of the holiday season vowing to take over the Panama Canal, calling for the U.S. to buy Greenland from Denmark and suggesting Canada could become the 51st state. It all suggests Trump is focused on somehow expanding the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Musk writes op-ed backing far-right German political party
item.description: The Tesla CEO said he has the “right” to address the country’s political climate as he has “made significant investments” in Germany’s technological and industrial sectors.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he is a 'believer' in H-1B visas for skilled migrant workers as right spars on immigration: report
item.description: President-elect Trump appeared to agree with Elon Musk in supporting immigration visas for highly skilled workers, as the right splits on the ongoing H-1B visa conversation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michelle Obama sparks backlash after posting 'Happy Holidays' video
item.description: Pro-Trump critics slammed Michelle Obama for her "Happy Holidays" message on Instagram, which said the past few months have been "difficult" since President-elect Trump won.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to delay TikTok ban so he can weigh in after he takes office
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump asked the Supreme Court on Friday to pause the potential TikTok ban from going into effect until his administration can pursue a “political resolution” to the issue.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge signals that contempt hearing for Rudy Giuliani over his assets might not go well for him
item.description: A federal judge is signaling that Rudy Giuliani's contempt hearing next Friday might not end so well for the former New York City mayor and onetime personal lawyer for President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Janet Yellen tells Congress US could hit debt limit in mid-January
item.description: Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen says her agency will need to start taking “extraordinary measures,” or special accounting maneuvers intended to prevent the nation from hitting the debt ceiling, as early as January 14th, in a letter sent to congressional leaders Friday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says fate of TikTok should be in his hands when he returns to White House
item.description: President-elect Trump said in an amicus brief Friday that he should make the decision as to whether TikTok can continue to operate in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More Americans say they need a break from political news: Survey
item.description: Most adults want a break from political news due to overload and fatigue, according to a new survey from the Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Congressional lawmakers almost got a pay bump this year. Then it crashed and burned
item.description: Lawmakers on Capitol Hill haven’t had a pay increase since 2009, representing a 31 percent decline in their salary when adjusted for inflation.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: The US says it pushed retraction of a famine warning for north Gaza. Aid groups express concern
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A lead organization monitoring for food crises around the world withdrew a new report this week warning of imminent famine in north Gaza under what it called Israel's “near-total blockade,” after the U.S. asked for its retraction, U.S. officials told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bald eagle finally becomes official US bird
item.description: The eagle had long been associated with the U.S., but it only just officially became the national bird.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Biden to leave White House with little fanfare
item.description: Editor's note: President Biden commuted the sentences of 37 people on federal death row to life in prison. President Biden is readying to leave the White House with little fanfare.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump faces roadblocks to ambitious border crackdown
item.description: President-elect Trump has made ambitious promises on the border and immigration, including the biggest deportation operation ever seen. But much like in Trump’s first term in office, his immigration agenda faces significant roadblocks.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden takes heat for vetoing bill that would add new judgeships
item.description: Federal judiciary leadership and some lawmakers are criticizing President Biden for vetoing a bill that would’ve added 66 new federal judgeships amid growing caseloads.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump vows to direct DOJ to pursue death penalty in wake of Biden clemencies
item.description: President-elect Trump vowed Tuesday to direct his Department of Justice (DOJ) to pursue the death penalty in the wake of President Biden’s clemencies of nearly every federal death row inmate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Opinion: Elon Musk wants to ‘delete’ many Americans' financial lifeline
item.description: It is especially surprising that one of the first federal agencies to come under scrutiny from the incoming administration is one that has returned billions of dollars to many of the same consumers who were counting on leaders in Washington to look out for their wallets.
item.dc:creator: Sharon McGowan, opinion contributor, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bill Clinton discharged after hospitalization for flu
item.description: Former President Clinton was discharged Tuesday morning after being hospitalized a day earlier for flu, according to his spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: As costs rise, number of Minnesotans losing heat, electricity spikes
item.description: The Citizens Utility Board of Minnesota, a nonprofit that advocates for utility customers, tracks reported shutoffs. It found utilities disconnected more than 85,000 residential households from either gas or electric services from January through October.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Bill Clinton hospitalized with fever but in good spirits, spokesperson says
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton was admitted Monday to Georgetown University Medical Center in Washington after developing a fever.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ethics releases Matt Gaetz report: 5 takeaways
item.description: The report — which spans 42 pages and includes several more in exhibits — found “substantial evidence” that Gaetz violated House rules, state and federal laws, outlining allegations of prostitution, statutory rape and illicit drug use, among other accusations.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: READ: House Ethics report on Gaetz allegations
item.description: The House Ethics Committee on Monday released its report on former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), who has faced allegations of sexual misconduct and illicit drug use, among other violations.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, Fox 5

item.title: Biden gives life in prison to 37 of 40 federal death row inmates
item.description: The move spares the lives of people convicted in killings, including the slayings of police and military officers, people on federal land and those involved in deadly bank robberies or drug deals, as well as the killings of guards or prisoners in federal facilities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gaetz sues Ethics Committee in last-minute bid to block release of report
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) filed a lawsuit Monday against the House Ethics Committee in a last-minute attempt to block the panel from releasing its report on him.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Biden commutes sentences of 37 federal death row prisoners
item.description: Opponents of the death penalty had urged President Joe Biden to take this step, given the number of executions that took place during President-elect Donald Trump's first term.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Twenty years after first proposed, NewRange still vying to build Minnesota’s first copper mine
item.description: Nearly 20 years after it was first proposed to state and federal regulators, the project formerly known as PolyMet Mining — now NewRange Copper Nickel — soldiers on, despite environmental groups’ contention that the project in its current form is dead.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Broken tie in Minnesota House would give GOP muscle at start of 2025 session
item.description: Even with a slight majority, Republicans could flex newfound power at the start of the legislative session if DFL lawmaker’s disqualification stands.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News
